questions.question: Capstone Helm values setup — что критично для production?

options.text: executor='CeleryExecutor,KubernetesExecutor' (mixed), 2 schedulers + 2 triggerers + 2 webservers HA, dagProcessor standalone (3.x preview), PgBouncer transaction mode, External Secrets через Vault, anti-affinity для разнести pods, KEDA для worker autoscale, network policies, per-component DB users

questions.explanation: Production-grade Helm values: HA для каждого critical component (2 schedulers/triggerers/webservers), mixed executors, PgBouncer mandatory, External Secrets (Vault), KEDA для cost-effective autoscaling, network policies (security), anti-affinity (spread по nodes — survives node loss), per-component DB users (defense in depth). Built-in Postgres/Redis в Helm — anti-pattern в production (single-node, no backup).

questions.question: Multiple Executors (CeleryExecutor + KubernetesExecutor) adds operational complexity. Стоит ли it оф?

options.text: Worthwhile когда clear mix workloads — light tasks + heavy tasks. Capstone case: consume_kafka light/frequent → Celery; spark_transform heavy/rare → K8s. Single executor compromise (Celery only → OOM heavy; K8s only → 10s overhead light). Complexities: dual debug paths, double network policies, monitoring split — manageable trade-off для real benefits

questions.explanation: AIP-61 (2.10+) Multiple Executors добавляет complexity: routing decisions, debugging dual paths, monitoring 2 systems, NetworkPolicy для обоих executors. Justified когда: clear light/heavy split (capstone), need custom resources per task (GPUs), gradual migration к K8s. NOT justified: tiny deployments (<50 DAGs), homogeneous workloads. Migration к 3.x: AIP-61 supported + Edge Executor (AIP-69) addition. Capstone setup future-proof.

questions.question: KEDA для Celery workers — что даёт vs static replicas?

options.text: Autoscale based на actual Celery queue length, не CPU. HPA не видит queue. KEDA: idle → 2 workers (min), 100 queued → 10 workers (100/10), 200+ queued → 16 max. Cost saving: pay только за actual workload. Static replicas = либо overprovisioned (idle waste) либо underprovisioned (tasks queued)

questions.explanation: KEDA superior к HPA для Celery: HPA scales по CPU/memory (workers могут быть idle при queued tasks → HPA не scale up). KEDA через redis-trigger смотрит на actual queue length — direct indicator workload. Production: minReplicaCount=2 (always-on responsiveness), maxReplicaCount=16+ (scale up bursts), listLength=10 tasks per worker. Cost saving substantial — typical 50-70% reduction worker hours vs static.

questions.question: Anti-affinity для scheduler pods через preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution — обязательно для HA.
questions.explanation: Без anti-affinity Kubernetes может schedule оба scheduler pods на same node — если node dies, оба scheduler lost, HA не работает. podAntiAffinity с topologyKey=kubernetes.io/hostname spreads pods across nodes. preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution — soft constraint (best-effort), не block scheduling если impossible. Hard variant — requiredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution. Production: preferred обычно sufficient + достаточно nodes. Same applies для triggerer, webserver pods.
